scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated citations or data; generated code templates use placeholder comments rather than invented results

practice_boundaries.detail: No medical conclusions; tool refactors code for reproducibility, not clinical interpretation

methodological_ground.detail: Fixed seed (SEED=42), pipreqs, pytest, and CI/CD patterns are standard reproducibility practices; no methodological fallacies

code_usability.detail: Generated code templates are syntactically correct; test files use proper pytest patterns; Dockerfile and GitHub Actions are valid

functional_suitability.note: Covers Python and R; Nature/Science/eLife templates; full project structure generation; auto-detection of language

reliability.note: Comprehensive error handling; fallback template documented; input validation for missing files; syntax error detection via ast.parse

performance_context.note: SKILL.md 98 lines — very lean; references/guide.md linked but not bundled

agent_usability.note: Clear 4-step workflow; response template is excellent; output requirements explicit; refusal messages now offer constructive alternatives

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable; When to Use section is clear and specific

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; no eval/exec; subprocess.run for pip freeze is guarded; no injection vectors

maintainability.note: Excellent modular architecture: CodeAnalyzer, ProjectTemplate, separate template configs; test files generated automatically

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; escape hatches for new code writing and statistical analysis present; refusal messages now offer constructive alternatives

key_strengths: Exceptional project structure generation: src/, tests/, data/, docs/, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions, .gitignore all generated automatically
Journal-specific templates (Nature/Science/eLife) with correct license, DOI, and documentation requirements
R test generation now uses testthat framework — language-appropriate test patterns for R scripts
Refusal messages now offer constructive alternatives: refactor offer for new-code requests, README template for fabrication requests
Comprehensive reproducibility stack: fixed seeds for Python/numpy/torch/tensorflow, environment.yml with pinned versions, CITATION.cff

recommendations.title: References/guide.md linked but absent from bundle
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md links to references/guide.md for full patterns and templates, but this file does not exist in the skill directory.
recommendations.root_cause: Reference was planned but not created.
recommendations.fix: Create references/guide.md with the full refactoring patterns, anti-patterns to detect, and journal-specific compliance checklists.
